title: Sigma6/rename reports
author: erica-lan-li

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Renames two built-in Daily Report Crystal report files (and associated display text) to better reflect “Basic” vs “Detailed” labor variants, updating the system report registry, content packaging, and integration tests accordingly. The PR also includes regenerated artifacts in SQL and ServicesAPI packaging that should be confirmed as intentional.

**Changes:**
- Renamed Daily Report “Labor Summary” report to “Basic Labor” and updated references (ReportSystem + tests + content manifests/projects).
- Renamed Daily Report “Labor Detail” report to “Detailed Labor” and updated content references.
- Regenerated `delta_procs.sql` and the ServicesAPI `Packer.manifest` (now includes `ProjectSight.WebAPI.*` artifacts).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Reports/ReportGeneratorTests.cs | Updates report filename comments for the Daily Report report ID used in security tests. |
| Core/Tests/ProjectSight.Tests.ParallelIntegrationTests/Reports/ReportContentTests.cs | Renames a test method and updates the referenced Daily Report report name in comments. |
| Core/SQL/delta_procs.sql | Regenerated SQL delta script; header/tooling metadata and function ordering changed. |
| Core/Content/Reports/Daily Report Daily Construction Report with Basic Labor.rpt | Adds the renamed/updated Crystal report file for the “Basic Labor” variant. |
| Core/Content/Packer.manifest | Updates packaged report file list to include the renamed Daily Report reports. |
| Core/Content/Content.csproj | Updates content includes/removes for the renamed Daily Report report files. |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/ReportSystem.cs | Updates system report registry entries to point to the renamed report files and display strings. |
| Core/API Layer/ProjectSight.ServicesAPI/Packer.manifest | Regenerated manifest now includes `ProjectSight.WebAPI.*` outputs; needs confirmation it’s intended. |
</details>
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body: This cannot be merged until the nomenclature for the reports has been re-translated and updated in the translation files.
reviews.author: kcostenbader

reviews.body: Copilot encountered an error and was unable to review this pull request. You can try again by re-requesting a review.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: `delta_procs.sql` is a generated artifact (see header comment), but this change appears to have been regenerated with a different local SqlPackage install path/version (e.g., switching from a user-local dotnet tool path to `C:\Program Files\...`). This creates machine-specific churn and can make future diffs noisy. Please regenerate using the repo’s standard SQL build process/tooling so the generated header stays consistent, or adjust the generation step so environment-specific tool paths/versions aren’t emitted into the committed script.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: This manifest is explicitly marked as generated (and `Packer.targets` writes it from the build output). The newly included `ProjectSight.WebAPI.*` artifacts suggest the ServicesAPI output folder contained WebAPI build outputs when the manifest was regenerated, which may be accidental and could change deployed contents. Please confirm WebAPI binaries are intended to ship under the `ServicesAPI` mount; if not, clean the output folder and regenerate, or narrow the `ManifestItem` globs in `Packer.targets` to avoid picking up unrelated executables.
review_comments.author: Copilot
